title: oklch()
description: Defines colors in the OKLab perceptual color space using Lightness, Chroma, and Hue. Unlike hex or hsl(), rotating the hue produces colors of equal perceived brightness — no manual brightness tuning required. Baseline newly available since 2023.
html:
30°oklch(65% 0.2 30)
145°your answer here
260°oklch(65% 0.2 260)

All three share the same lightness (65%) and chroma (0.2) — only hue differs
challenge: The first (orange) and third (blue) swatches already use oklch() with identical lightness and chroma. The middle swatch is a gray placeholder. Set its background to oklch(65% 0.2 145) to complete the perceptually even palette with a green.

alternatives.name: oklch() with an alpha channel
alternatives.description: A fourth argument adds transparency. oklch(65% 0.2 260 / 0.5) is a 50% transparent blue — more predictable than rgba() because the lightness is perceptually uniform even at partial opacity.

realWorldUsage: Generating color palettes and scales in design tokens — oklch() lets you define a consistent tonal palette by stepping the hue value, confident each step will look equally bright. It's increasingly used in design systems replacing HSL, which has well-known perceived-brightness inconsistencies across hues.
